wbsp26_03_p17_continuity-explanation: There is G(1) continuity at the cat’s mouth. The mouth is made from two cubic Bézier segments. They meet at the point (100, 20). The last control point of the first segment and the first control point of the second segment are both at (100, 40). Because these control points are aligned vertically with the shared endpoint, the tangent directions at the connection are the same. This creates a smooth transition between the two curves without any visible corner, demonstrating G(1) continuity.
wbsp26_03_ainote: I used AI tools to help debug my JavaScript code, improve formatting, and optimize the animation logic for the aquarium scene.
wbsp26_03_p24_train-features:
How to Run:

Start the train: Click the checkbox above the slider to run the animation.

Toggle features: Use the checkboxes below the canvas to enable or disable advanced and creative features in real time.

Key Features (Rubric Highlights):

Standard Features

Cardinal Spline Track: The train follows a cardinal cubic spline.

Control Points: Click and drag to move points; Shift-Click to add, Ctrl-Click to delete.

Train Direction: The train automatically rotates to point along the tangent of the track.

Arc-Length Issue Shown Initially: The initial layout demonstrates non-uniform speed along segments of varying length.

Advanced Features

Arc-Length Parameterization: When enabled, the train moves at a constant speed.

Track Details: Wooden rail ties are automatically aligned perpendicular to the track; steel rails are drawn as parallel offset curves.

Variations: Supports switching between cardinal splines and smooth B-Spline curves, as well as multiple connected train cars.

Creative Features

Dynamic Smoke: The engine emits animated smoke.

Enhanced Train Appearance: The train model is visually detailed.

Panoramic Scenery: Includes clouds, trees, houses, and landscape elements for a more immersive environment.
wbsp26_03_p26_explanation:
This program creates an animated aquarium with curves:

Curved shapes: The tank, fish, and swaying plants are drawn with Bézier curves.

Curved motion: Fish swim along elliptical paths inside the tank; plants sway naturally with sine-based movement.

Visual effect: Bright, clear colors give a summery feel, and everything stays inside the tank.
